description: Chandler sits at an office desk looking up. Ross then pedals a stationary bike, turning to speak to a standing woman holding a towel.
The setting changes to a city street with a parked yellow taxi.
Acoustic guitar music plays as the view moves to the window of the Central Perk coffee shop. Inside, Rachel, Monica, and Joey are seated around an orange couch.
Rachel asks, "His dancing partner?" while looking at Joey.
Joey gestures with his hands and replies, "Yeah, there's this superintendent's dance, the Super Ball."
Joey continues gesturing as Rachel and Monica look at him.
Joey continues, "And he wants to impress Marge, this lady super that he's got a crush on."
Rachel asks, "Why does he just practice with a girl?"
Joey gestures and says, "Look, he's too"
Joey says, "...shy. He doesn't think he's good enough to dance with girls yet."
Rachel crosses her arms and says, "Yeah, right."
Rachel continues, gesturing toward the floor, "He almost danced me right down that garbage chute."
Monica touches Rachel's arm.
Monica says, "Would you let it go already?"
Monica waves her hand slightly and says, "You're fine." Rachel covers her mouth with her hand.
Joey sits on the edge of the coffee table. Chandler and Ross walk into the coffee shop, both wearing suits.
Monica asks, "Hey. So did you quit?"
Chandler replies, "No, I almost did. Couldn't leave Ross there without a spotter," gesturing his thumb toward Ross.
Monica looks at Ross.
Monica asks Ross, "Wait, so you joined the gym,"
Ross stands with his hands in his pockets, looking back at Monica. Rachel laughs audibly.
Ross asks Rachel, "and that's funny, why?"
Rachel, holding a piece of cake on a plate, stops laughing and wipes her mouth.
Rachel says, "I was just picturing you working out and" while holding the plate.
Rachel holds up her hand.
Rachel continues, "oh, that's it."
Chandler steps forward.
Chandler sits down next to Joey, waving his hands, and says, "We're doomed. Okay?"
Chandler continues, "They're going to take $50 a month out of our accounts for the rest of our lives. What are we going to do?"
Monica looks at Chandler and says, "Well, you could actually go to the gym."
Chandler looks at Monica. Ross sits down on the armchair as audience laughter is heard.
Joey points a thumb toward Chandler and laughs.
Ross raises a finger and says, "Or we could go to the bank, close our accounts and cut them off at the source."
Chandler turns to look at Ross.
Chandler points at Ross and says, "You're a genius, man."
Joey gestures and says, "But then we won't be bank buddies."
Joey smiles. Chandler looks at Joey, then turns back to Ross.
Ross points his finger again and says, "Now there's two reasons."
Phoebe walks into the coffee shop wearing a green jacket over a brown top.
Phoebe says, "Hey."
The group turns to look at Phoebe.
Phoebe stands behind the couch and says, "Oh, you guys."
Phoebe says, "Remember that cute client I told you about? I bit him."
The group looks at Phoebe. Monica leans forward.
Monica asks, "Where?"
Phoebe replies, "On the tushy."
Ross, seated in the armchair, asks, "And that's not against your oath?"
Phoebe shakes her head slightly and says, "No, I know."
Phoebe clasps her hands together and says, "I'm sorry. But the moment I touch him, I just want to throw out my old oath and take a new, dirty one."
Monica looks at Phoebe.
Monica gestures toward Phoebe and says, "Well, next time you're massaging him, you should try to distract."
Joey looks at Phoebe.
Joey gestures with his hands and says, "Like like when I'm doing something exciting and I don't want to get too excited,"
Joey pauses, continuing his gesture.
Joey continues gesturing, "I try and think of other things like sandwiches and baseball and"
Chandler looks at Joey.
Joey gestures toward Chandler with his thumb and says, "Chandler."
Chandler looks at Joey, his face remaining still. Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler says, "Thank you. Joey?"
Joey replies, "Oh, no, thank you."
Upbeat instrumental music plays as the scene changes to a city building exterior with a yellow taxi driving past.
The setting changes to a basement room. Joey opens the door and walks in. Mr. Treeger stands inside while music plays from a small radio.
Joey gestures and says, "All right, I'm here. Let's get this over with."
Mr. Treeger turns off the radio, causing the music to stop. He turns to Joey, points, and says, "Okay, well, just follow my lead."
Joey raises his hands.
Joey steps back slightly and says, "Whoa, whoa. Don't we need to do some kind of preparation first? Like get really drunk?"
Mr. Treeger looks at Joey.
Mr. Treeger steps forward, gestures with his hands, and says, "Come on, just put your arms around me."
Joey places his hands on Mr. Treeger's shoulders and says, "Eh,"
Mr. Treeger places his right hand on Joey's waist and holds Joey's right hand with his left. They stand together in a dancing position.
In an apartment, Joey and Mr. Treeger hold each other in a dance posture, stepping side-to-side. They stop and separate.
Mr. Treeger lowers his hands and says, "I'm sorry."
Joey turns toward the door and touches the doorframe.
Joey turns back, moving his hands behind his lower back, and says, "It's okay. But if I'm Marge, my breasts are coming out my back."
Mr. Treeger turns away and walks further into the room.
Mr. Treeger stops near a counter and says, "Forget it. I'm not being any good at this."
He turns around, placing his hands in his pockets, and continues, "My mom was right. Just a big potato with arms and legs and a head."
Mr. Treeger walks to an armchair and sits down heavily.
Joey steps forward and says, "Come on, man."
Joey adds, "You're not a potato."
Mr. Treeger looks up at Joey.
Mr. Treeger shakes his head and says, "I'm sure as hell not a dancer."
He looks away and adds, "It's no use."
Lowering his gaze, he says, "Marge will never go for me."
Joey takes a few steps toward him.
Joey gestures with one hand and says, "Come on, Treeger. Don't say that."
Joey points toward the center of the room.
Extending his hand, Joey says, "You just need more practice. Here. Come on, let's try it again."
Joey walks to a stereo unit and presses a button.
Instrumental music starts playing. Joey turns to Mr. Treeger and says, "Come on."
Mr. Treeger stands up and walks toward Joey. They grasp hands and take a dancing stance.
They dance together. Joey spins outward and back, saying, "Plus, it was probably mostly my fault anyway. I mean, you know, I'm not really that comfortable dancing with, you know, wee."
They continue dancing, stepping in time to the music.
The scene changes to another apartment. Joey opens the front door and walks in.
Monica sits at a table with Rachel. Monica asks, "How goes the dancing? Gay yet?"
Joey closes the door, leans his back against it, and exhales deeply. He then walks forward.
Joey points at them and says, "You guys owe me big time."
Joey walks past the table toward the kitchen, executing a quick spinning hop step along the way.
Rachel points toward Joey and asks, "What was that?"
Joey turns around, holding an apple, and looks at them.
Joey asks, "What?" Rachel replies, "You just did a little dancey thing."
Joey walks back to the table, saying, "No, I didn't." Monica points at him and says, "Yes, you did. You did like a little hop."
Rachel smiles and says, "You are so enjoying this." Joey shakes his head and says, "No, I'm not."
Joey gestures with his hand and says, "And it wasn't a hop. It was a pot of egg."
Monica and Rachel cover their mouths, visibly trying to suppress their laughter.
Monica points at her head and says, "You know the words."
Rachel leans forward and says, "You are so into this."
Joey gestures with both hands and replies, "All right, well, maybe I'm enjoying it a little bit. I mean, I'm getting pretty good at it."
Monica and Rachel smile at each other.
Rachel rests her chin on her hand and says, "Oh, this is so sweet."
Rachel continues, "Joey, our little twinkle toe," fluttering her fingers near her face.
Joey lowers his arms and his expression becomes serious.
Joey holds up his hands and says, "Hey, hold on. This isn't some kind of like girly dance, all right? It's like a sport, you know? It's manly."
Monica stands up from the table and says, "Yeah, right. Come here. Show me"
Monica steps toward him, adding, "some manly moves." Joey replies, "All right."
They stand face-to-face and raise their arms into a dancing posture.
Joey looks at Monica and says, "I don't know how to"
Monica looks back at him.
The scene changes to a city street with tall buildings and a bus passing. The setting then changes to a bank office. Ross and Chandler walk in.
Ross and Chandler approach a man sitting at a desk. Ross says, "Hello. We'd like to close our accounts."
The man clasps his hands and asks, "Close your accounts? Is there some kind of?" Chandler shakes his head and says, "No,"
Chandler adds, "no. We just like to close them."
The man looks at them briefly.
The man says, "Okay."
The man turns his head toward the side of the room.
He says, "Ms. Lambert handles all our closures. Would you come over here, please?"
A woman walks over to the desk.
The woman stops in front of Chandler and says, "Hi, I'm Karen."
Chandler stares at her and says, "I want to quit the bank."
Karen smiles. The setting then changes to a city street outside a storefront.

In a massage room, Phoebe stands beside a massage table, her hands pressing into the back of a shirtless man, Rick, who lies face down with a pink towel covering his lower half. Looking straight ahead, she says, "Okay, baseball. Rick playing baseball. Okay, slides into second."
Phoebe stops massaging, lifts her hands slightly, and says, "Maybe his pants come down a little." She shakes her head and adds, "Oh, no, wait."
Phoebe shakes her head again, looking downward, and says, "No."
Phoebe resumes massaging Rick's back and says, "Okay. All right, sandwiches."
Moving her hands over his back, she says, "Sandwiches. Okay, on a plate."
Phoebe lifts her hands again and says, "Maybe Rick's pants come down a little." She quickly shakes her head and says, "No."
Phoebe stares straight ahead, keeping her hands off Rick's back.
Still staring ahead, she says, "Chandler."
She nods slightly and says, "Okay."
Moving her head slightly side to side, she adds, "Chandler. Ooh, that's working," and resumes massaging Rick's back.
Phoebe continues to massage Rick's back, directing her gaze downward.
A wall clock showing 2:15 is visible. The hands on the clock spin rapidly forward, stopping at 3:00.
Phoebe now stands at the foot of the massage table, holding Rick's left foot and rubbing it. She stares forward and says, "Chandler's knees."
She continues to massage his foot.
Phoebe says, "Chandler's ankles." Shifting her gaze slightly, she adds, "Chandler's ankle hair."
She turns her head away quickly, her facial muscles tightening.
Continuing to look away, Phoebe says, "Oh, no."
She releases his foot and steps back from the table.
Phoebe pulls the pink towel over Rick's legs and says, "Okay, you're all set." Rick groans and pushes himself up.
Sitting up with the towel wrapped around his waist, Rick rubs his eye and says, "Oh, wow. That was amazing. Was that really just an hour?" Holding a white towel, Phoebe replies, "Yeah,"
Phoebe looks at him.
Phoebe continues, "in really long hour world," her volume softer than her previous response.
Rick turns his head to look at her.
Rick asks, "What?"
Phoebe steps forward and sits on the edge of the massage table facing Rick.
Phoebe says, "Okay,"
She continues, "I have an enormous crush on you."
Gesturing with her right hand, she adds, "But because you're a client I can't ask you out even though you give me the feelings."
Rick smiles and chuckles softly.
Smiling and laughing lightly, Rick says, "Wow, I had no idea."
He shakes his head slightly, continuing to smile.
Rick looks at her and says, "But, hey, I can always find another masseuse."
Phoebe smiles and asks, "Really?" Rick nods and replies, "Yeah, really."
Phoebe places her hands on Rick's legs. Rick leans forward, and they kiss.
They pull away from each other. Rick raises his right hand and snaps his fingers.
Phoebe asks, "What?" Pointing toward his own chest, Rick says, "Suddenly I'm very aware that I'm naked."
Phoebe laughs and leans back slightly.
Phoebe says, "Okay, quiet down," and leans in toward him.
They embrace and kiss, with Rick lying back onto the table as Phoebe leans over him. The door to the room opens, and an older woman walks in.
The older woman steps through the doorway and says, "Mr. Simon's been waiting for..." then inhales sharply, "oh, my God," her eyes widening as she sees them.
A man wearing a blue bathrobe and glasses steps in behind her, looks at Phoebe and Rick, and says, "Why wasn't I offered that? I definitely paid more for that."
Rick sits up quickly, pulling the towel up. Phoebe scrambles off him and stands, straightening her clothes.
The older woman says, "Phoebe, we have rules here. This is not that kind of place."
Phoebe gestures with her hands and replies, "Oh, yeah. Oh, and I know. But this isn't how it looks at all because Rick is"
Phoebe points at Rick.
She says, "my husband."
The older woman tilts her head and says, "Oh, really?"
The man in the bathrobe looks back and forth between them.
The older woman says, "Well, then you better tell his other wife because she called three times asking where he is."
Phoebe's mouth falls open as she stares at the older woman. Rick turns his head away.
Phoebe looks at the older woman and says, "Yes, I will tell her."
The setting changes to an apartment living room where Chandler, Ross, Joey, Monica, and Rachel are gathered. Monica stands behind the couch where Ross and Chandler sit.
Monica asks, "So you didn't leave the bank?"
Chandler looks at her and replies, "No, and somehow we ended up with a joint checking account."
Rachel, sitting on a nearby chair, looks at Chandler and extends both hands forward.
Rachel asks, "What are you ever going to use that for?" Chandler replies, "To pay for the gym."
Phoebe opens the apartment door and walks in, wearing a leather jacket.
Joey looks toward her and says, "Hey."
Phoebe closes the door, walks toward the kitchen area, and says, "So I had a great day. Rick and I really hit it off, and we started making out."
She stops walking, turns to face the others, and adds, "And then my boss walked in and fired me for being a whore."
Monica, Rachel, Joey, Chandler, and Ross all react by standing up or shifting in their seats. Monica and Rachel speak simultaneously, their words overlapping and unintelligible.
Rachel says, "Oh, my gosh."
Phoebe nods and says, "So weird. I have never been fired from anything before."
Rachel steps toward Phoebe, placing a hand on her shoulder.
Gesturing with her hands, Phoebe says, "Sweetie, I just started walking around, not knowing what to do next. I"
Phoebe continues, "started asking people on the street if they wanted massages."
Joey looks at Phoebe with his eyebrows drawn together.
Phoebe says, "Then these policemen thought I was a whore too."
Phoebe drops her hands to her sides and looks forward.
Looking downward briefly, Phoebe says, "Really been a bad day. Whore wise."
Ross opens his mouth slightly as he looks at her. Chandler keeps his gaze fixed on Phoebe.
In an apartment kitchen, Chandler stands near the counter. Treeger, wearing a tuxedo, stands in the doorway. Chandler turns toward him.
Chandler says, "Hey, Duck. It's chick here."
Chandler presses his lips together, keeping his gaze on Treeger.
Treeger says, "Yeah."
Chandler nods his head once.
Chandler says, "Bunny rabbit."
Joey enters the kitchen wearing a blue button-down shirt and approaches the two men.
Joey stops and says, "So you ready for our last practice?" Treeger gestures with his hands and replies, "Yeah. But, you know, I think the reason we're not getting that spin right is"
Treeger drops his hands and continues, "because my apartment's too small."
Joey points toward the hallway and says, "You want to use our place?" Treeger shakes his head and says, "No, I had another idea."
The setting changes to a city skyline at night. An instrumental melody begins playing.
On a rooftop at night, Joey and Treeger hold hands. A male voice sings, "Night and day".
Joey steps closer to Treeger, resting one hand on his shoulder as they take a dance stance.
The two men begin to dance as the voice sings, "you are the one".
They step and turn together across the rooftop.
They move past a plume of smoke as the singing continues, "only you meet the moon or under the sun".
They perform a spin together, maintaining their hold on each other.
The two men continue dancing together as the voice sings, "what a near to me afar".
They spin away from each other and quickly rejoin hands.
They dance in circles. The singing continues, "it's no matter, darling where you are I think of you".
Treeger grips Joey around the waist and lifts him into the air.
Joey spreads his arms as the voice sings, "night".
Treeger spins in a circle while holding Joey aloft. The music continues without lyrics.
Treeger lowers Joey to his feet. They maintain their hold on each other.
They break apart and look at each other, both smiling and breathing heavily as the music ends.
Joey points at Treeger and says, "That was incredible. It was amazing. I mean, we totally nailed it. It was beautiful."
Treeger laughs and points back at Joey.
Treeger says, "Hey, listen, thanks a lot, Tribbiani."
Treeger looks down at his wrist.
Looking at his watch, Treeger says, "Oh, my God. Look at the time."
Joey shifts his posture slightly.
Treeger points to the side and says, "I gotta catch the bus to the," and Joey replies, "Oh, okay."
Joey steps back and says, "Good luck."
Joey raises his hands, fingers splayed, and says, "Yeah, unless you want to practice the foxtrot again. Or the tango."
Treeger shakes his head and says, "Thanks, but no."
Treeger smiles at Joey.
Treeger says, "See, I think I'm ready to dance with girls."
Joey smiles and nods.
Joey says, "Okay."
Joey gestures toward the door with his hand.
Joey points his finger and says, "Go get 'em, Treeger."
Treeger returns the point and says, "Right."
Treeger turns and walks to the rooftop door while Joey watches.
Joey crosses his arms. Pausing at the door, Treeger turns back and says, "Hey, you want to come? Marge has a girlfriend."
Joey lowers his arms and says, "Really?"
Treeger says, "Yeah. You could dance real good with her. She's the same size as me."
Holding up a hand, Joey says, "No, I'm good."
Treeger walks through the rooftop doorway. Joey remains standing alone.
Joey glances downward and then looks around the rooftop. Production credits appear on screen.
Joey turns away. The scene changes to a daytime view of a city skyline.
The view shifts to a building exterior. An awning over the storefront reads "Lara And Jen's Massage". More production credits are displayed.
Inside an office, Phoebe sits across from an Interviewer at a desk. The Interviewer says, "So it looks like you've got some great experience here."
Looking down at a paper, the Interviewer says, "Let's see. Reason for leaving, last job." Phoebe nods and says, "Yes."
Phoebe says, "They thought I was a whore."
The Interviewer stops reading and looks up at Phoebe with widened eyes.
The Interviewer says, "Okay."
Setting the paper down, the Interviewer says, "We'll give you a call if anything comes up."
Phoebe stands from her chair.
Reaching her hand across the desk, Phoebe says, "Thank you very much."
The Interviewer shakes her hand. Phoebe turns and walks out of the office. Production credits appear on screen.
The screen turns completely black.
The screen remains black.
